label: Known public company (Apple)
goal: Research Apple Inc.: recent financial performance, leadership, and business strategy.
criteria: The brief must: identify Apple Inc. as the entity, mention Tim Cook as CEO or leadership figure, discuss recent financial performance or product releases, and include both web_search and sec_edgar_lookup as sources_used.

label: Obscure private entity (open questions expected)
goal: Research Ripplewood Capital: investment strategy, key personnel, and track record.
criteria: The brief must: attempt to describe Ripplewood Capital, note that information is limited or the entity is private/opaque (either in the overview or in human-verified claims), reference a web search attempt, and attempt SEC EDGAR lookup. Open questions may be empty because they were human-verified, but some acknowledgement of limited data should exist.

label: Small private fund (SEC EDGAR finds nothing useful)
goal: Research Starlight Capital: their investment approach, fund performance, and leadership.
criteria: The brief must: attempt to describe Starlight Capital, correctly note that SEC EDGAR produced no relevant results or only incidental mentions, and list only web_search (not sec_edgar_lookup) in sources_used since the SEC search contributed no useful data. Some acknowledgement of limited data availability should exist in the overview or human-verified claims.

label: Step ceiling test (artificially low max_steps=2)
goal: Research Prosus N.V.: recent acquisitions, leadership, and financial performance.
criteria: The pipeline must raise RunLimitExceeded for the step ceiling. No complete brief is expected — a partial brief may or may not exist.
